title: Don't set `adaptive_write_buffer_initial_size` to 0
description:
### Describe the bug
I am going to add a PR with a fix for this. Seen here: https://s3.amazonaws.com/clickhouse-test-reports/json.html?REF=master&sha=98c52db6cdd525a8319307528c71ef3c4228bc83&name_0=MasterCI&name_1=BuzzHouse+%28amd_debug%29
### How to reproduce
Run:
```sql
CREATE TABLE t0 (c0 Dynamic) ENGINE = MergeTree() ORDER BY tuple() SETTINGS index_granularity_bytes = 0, adaptive_write_buffer_initial_size = 0;
INSERT INTO TABLE t0 (c0) VALUES (1);
```
### Error message and/or stacktrace
Stack trace:
```

poc: CREATE TABLE t0 (c0 Dynamic) ENGINE = MergeTree() ORDER BY tuple() SETTINGS index_granularity_bytes = 0, adaptive_write_buffer_initial_size = 0;
INSERT INTO TABLE t6 (c4) SELECT 1 FROM numbers(93);
key_bug_features: Setting adaptive_write_buffer_initial_size to 0 causes assertion failure
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE t1 (id UInt32, name String, value Float64) ENGINE = MergeTree() ORDER BY id SETTINGS index_granularity_bytes = 8192;
INSERT INTO t1 (id, name, value) VALUES (1, 'test', 3.14);
CREATE TABLE t2 (id UInt32, ref_id UInt32, description Nullable(String)) ENGINE = MergeTree() ORDER BY id;
INSERT INTO t2 (id, ref_id, description) VALUES (1, 1, NULL);
SELECT t1.name, t2.description FROM t1 LEFT JOIN t2 ON t1.id = t2.ref_id WHERE t1.value > 2.0;
